title: dacrycarpus papuana
intro: Dacrycarpus papuana, also known as the Queen's Pine or Dacrydium papuanum, is a species of coniferous tree native to the tropical rainforests of New Guinea and surrounding islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Dacrycarpus papuana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against infections. The herbal preparations of Dacrycarpus papuana typically involve boiling the tree's bark or leaves in water to create a decoction that can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical application.
